commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Human review is needed because external live behavior proof is missing and maintainers should decide whether the sibling profile-import contract issue must be included before merge.; Cleared: The diff changes Nostr channel runtime/tests only and does not add dependencies, lockfile changes, permissions, scripts, credential handling, or new execution surfaces. (sha=e8ac1877ea6cfd2da31f4b48b4a999d51335d5fe)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the Nostr plugin to pass a single DM filter object to `subscribeMany`, keep `startNostrGatewayAccount` pending until abort, and adds focused lifecycle/subscription regression tests.

PR surface: Source +15, Tests +113. Total +128 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source and linked logs: current main both array-wraps the DM filter and returns from `startAccount`, while the linked report shows strict-relay rejection and restart-loop symptoms. I did not run a live Nostr relay reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Human review is needed because external live behavior proof is missing and maintainers should decide whether the sibling profile-import contract issue must be included before merge.

target.title: [AI-assisted] fix(nostr): keep DM subscriptions alive until abort
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the Nostr channel could restart-loop because the DM subscription passed an array-wrapped filter to `nostr-tools` and the gateway account task returned immediately after startup.
- Solution: pass a single NIP-01 DM filter object to `subscribeMany` and keep the Nostr gateway account task pending until abort.
- What changed: updated the Nostr bus subscription shape, wrapped gateway startup in `runStoppablePassiveMonitor`, and added focused regression coverage for subscription shape and abort-driven lifecycle cleanup.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no generated dist patch, raw media fallback, config shape, migration, relay policy, auth, or plugin API behavior changes.

## Motivation

Issue #53858 is a P1 message-loss/crash-loop bug. Strict Nostr relays reject the nested filter shape, so affected accounts cannot reliably receive DMs, and the immediately resolved account task can feed the generic gateway supervisor restart path.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #53858
- Related #86141
- Related #42832
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Nostr account startup no longer sends an array-wrapped DM subscription filter, and the gateway account task no longer resolves immediately into the supervisor restart path.
- Real environment tested: disposable Linux OpenClaw checkout based on `origin/main` `65e2120f8c`, Node `v24.15.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
git diff --check
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/nostr/src/gateway.ts extensions/nostr/src/nostr-bus.ts extensions/nostr/src/nostr-bus.inbound.test.ts extensions/nostr/src/channel.lifecycle.test.ts extensions/nostr/src/channel.inbound.test.ts extensions/nostr/src/channel.outbound.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/nostr/src/nostr-bus.inbound.test.ts extensions/nostr/src/channel.lifecycle.test.ts extensions/nostr/src/channel.inbound.test.ts extensions/nostr/src/channel.outbound.test.ts
pnpm test:extension nostr
pnpm test:contracts:channels
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
CHECK:focused-nostr
Test Files 4 passed (4)
Tests 22 passed (22)

CHECK:test-extension-nostr
Test Files 13 passed (13)
Tests 273 passed (273)

CHECK:test-contracts-channels
[test] passed 4 Vitest shards in 98.12s
```

- Observed result after fix: the focused tests confirm `subscribeMany` receives one filter object, `startAccount` stays pending until abort, and abort closes the bus. The full Nostr extension lane and channel contract shards pass on the rebased patch.
- What was not tested: no live public or private Nostr relay was contacted, and no real Nostr private key or DM traffic was used.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):

```text
#53858 relay log before fix:
invalid type: sequence, expected struct Filter

Malformed request shape:
["REQ","sub:1",[{"kinds":[4],"#p":["..."],"since":1774430972}]]

Expected request shape:
["REQ","sub:1",{"kinds":[4],"#p":["..."],"since":1774430972}]
```

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `startNostrBus` passed an array-shaped DM filter into the pinned `nostr-tools` `subscribeMany(relays, filter, params)` contract, and `startNostrGatewayAccount` returned a stop handle immediately after bus startup.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing tests covered inbound/outbound behavior, but did not assert the exact `subscribeMany` filter shape or that the gateway account task remains pending until abort.
- Contributing context (if known): ClawSweeper identified the same focused fix shape on #53858, and previous related repair PR #86141 was closed unmerged by automation rather than rejected for direction.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `extensions/nostr/src/nostr-bus.inbound.test.ts`
  - `extensions/nostr/src/channel.lifecycle.test.ts`
  - `extensions/nostr/src/channel.inbound.test.ts`
  - `extensions/nostr/src/channel.outbound.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Nostr DM subscriptions are sent as one filter object, and `startAccount` does not resolve until abort while still closing the active bus on abort.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it covers the dependency-contract boundary that creates the malformed REQ payload and the gateway lifecycle boundary that can trigger restarts.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): no existing test asserted the nested-array subscription shape or pending-until-abort account lifecycle.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Affected Nostr accounts should stay running and receive strict-relay DM subscriptions instead of restart-looping after startup.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
startAccount -> start bus -> return stop handle -> supervisor sees clean exit
subscribeMany(relays, [{ filter }], params) -> relay receives nested filter array

After:
startAccount -> start bus -> wait for abort -> close bus once
subscribeMany(relays, { filter }, params) -> relay receives one NIP-01 filter object
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux disposable checkout
- Runtime/container: Node `v24.15.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Nostr bundled extension
- Relevant config (redacted): no real Nostr account, private key, relay endpoint, or DM traffic used

### Steps

1. Install dependencies in a fresh OpenClaw checkout based on the tested `origin/main` SHA.
2. Copy the patched Nostr source and test files into the checkout.
3. Run diff/format checks.
4. Run the focused Nostr subscription/lifecycle/inbound/outbound tests.
5. Run the full Nostr extension test lane.
6. Run shared channel contract tests.

### Expected

- The subscription regression test observes a single filter object passed to `subscribeMany`.
- The lifecycle regression tests observe `startAccount` pending until abort and closing the bus on abort.
- The full Nostr extension lane and channel contract shards pass.

### Actual

- After this patch, matches expected.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Verification on PR head `e8ac1877ea`:

```text
git diff --check
passed

pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/nostr/src/gateway.ts extensions/nostr/src/nostr-bus.ts extensions/nostr/src/nostr-bus.inbound.test.ts extensions/nostr/src/channel.lifecycle.test.ts extensions/nostr/src/channel.inbound.test.ts extensions/nostr/src/channel.outbound.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/nostr/src/nostr-bus.inbound.test.ts extensions/nostr/src/channel.lifecycle.test.ts extensions/nostr/src/channel.inbound.test.ts extensions/nostr/src/channel.outbound.test.ts
4 test files passed / 22 tests passed

pnpm test:extension nostr
13 test files passed / 273 tests passed

pnpm test:contracts:channels
4 Vitest shards passed
```

Note: live relay proof was not run. The proof is diagnostic runtime/source-contract proof through the real Nostr extension test path, with #53858 providing the before-fix strict-relay rejection log.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: single-object DM subscription filter, Nostr account task remains pending until abort, abort closes the active bus, already-aborted startup closes the bus, inbound reply pipeline still routes allowed DMs, outbound sends still route through the Nostr gateway adapter.
- Edge cases checked: already-aborted signal after bus startup, idempotent cleanup of active bus/metrics state, and direct inbound/outbound harness shutdown through abort.
- What you did **not** verify: live public or private Nostr relay traffic, real Nostr private keys, real DMs, or generated dist artifacts.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: keeping `startAccount` pending could leak bus state if abort cleanup does not run.
  - Mitigation: the fix uses the existing `runStoppablePassiveMonitor` helper and adds lifecycle coverage for normal abort and already-aborted startup.
- Risk: changing the Nostr subscription shape could affect tolerant relays that accepted the old nested filter.
  - Mitigation: the new shape matches the pinned `nostr-tools` contract and NIP-01 REQ filter structure; strict-relay behavior in #53858 requires this shape.
target.author: iwhatsskill
